outbreak_notes: THE SUSPECTED CASES ARE PROBABLE! sex only available for 34/36 cases

The team collected 13 serum samples from 19 suspect casepatients. Seven of the serum samples were collected within less than seven days of illness onset. Three of the four serum samples that were collected after 10 days of illness onset had detectable IgM and IgG Nipah virus antibodies and were identified as confirmed cases. Five (26%) of the 19 suspected case-patients, who resided in the same outbreak village and died during the outbreak period but before samples could be collected, were categorized as probable case
Not the original study to describe this outbreak, but only cites CDC's MMWR, which we wouldn't include in the review

Outbreak start date is the date of symptom onset of the index case and the end date is the release date of the cases who survived.

Date reported is when the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare in India declared the outbreak - the index case developed a fever on 29 August 2021. Outbreak source is possibly due to the consumption of an infected fruit, from the article: 'father of the index case regarding the consumption of fruit from the orchard near the house.'. However, the authors could not find viral RNA in the sample of bats they tests and hence could not directly link the source of infection to bats.Tested 19 symptomatic  and 40 asymptomatic primary contacts, 5 asymptomatic secondary contacts, and 61  non-epidemiologically linked suspected cases from neighbouring districts. All were negative.
